mnemonics.title: Pumping Lemma Conditions
mnemonics.content: Remember as 'XYZ': X = |xy| ≤ p (eXactly within p), Y = |y| > 0 (Yes non-empty), Z = xyⁱz ∈ L (Zero to infinity). Also think: 'PUMP: Partition Unambiguously Means Proof' - Partition w into x,y,z, Unambiguous conditions, Means must hold for all i, Proof by contradiction.

mnemonics.title: Myhill-Nerode Key Idea
mnemonics.content: Think 'FINITE = REGULAR'. If distinguishable strings go on forever → Not Regular. The more distinguishable strings you find, the closer you are to proving non-regularity.

mnemonics.title: Proving Non-Regularity Steps
mnemonics.content: SARA: Suppose L is regular, Assume pumping length p, Choose specific string w, Apply lemma, Reject/contradiction

acronyms.expansion: Pumping Lemma - Universal condition, Must hold, Proves non-regularity
acronyms.usage: Remember conditions: Partition, |xy| ≤ p, |y| > 0, Must work for all i
x R_L y iff for all z, xz ∈ L ⇔ yz ∈ L
Method for Pumping Lemma proofs: Suppose L is regular, Assume pumping length p, Reject by finding counterexample, Apply contradiction

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a string w as a long train with first p cars (pumpable zone) containing engine x and fuel car y, followed by rest z. You can add/remove y cars (pump) and train still works (in language).

Think of equivalence classes as boxes. Finite automaton = finite boxes. If you need infinite boxes (distinguishable strings), you need more than finite states = non-regular. Draw infinite line of strings where each needs its own box.
Draw p as a boundary. String aᵖbᵖ crosses it. The 'a' portion that gets pumped must stay all 'a' (can't cross b boundary). After pumping, 'a' and 'b' counts won't match → contradiction.

analogies.analogy: Like a police breathalyzer: if you're over the limit (|w| ≥ p), there must be some segment (y) that can be repeated (pumped) and you'd still fail (not in L). If you can find a case where repeating fails, you're not drunk (not regular).

Think of strings as people and suffixes z as questions. Two strings are indistinguishable if no question can tell them apart about being in L. Finite automaton = finite memory = can only answer finitely many ways = finite equivalence classes.

Regular languages are like simple counters (few states). Language like aⁿbⁿ requires a real counter (unbounded) - like counting with fingers but need infinite fingers. Finite states can't do this, hence non-regular.
